name: Creation Order
shortDescription: The Creation Order in Islam refers to the sequence of events and entities brought into existence by Allah. It highlights the divine plan and the hierarchy within creation.
description: In Islamic cosmology, the Creation Order is a complex and divinely orchestrated sequence. It begins with Allah, the ultimate creator, who existed before all things. The exact details of the creation order are debated among scholars, but a general understanding exists. Before the physical universe, it is believed that Allah created the Pen (al-Qalam) and the Tablet (al-Lawh al-Mahfuz), where all events are recorded. Then, Allah created water and the Throne ('Arsh). Subsequently, the angels, including Jibreel (Gabriel), were created from light. Jinn were created from smokeless fire before humans. Finally, Adam, the first human, was created from clay. The creation of the universe, including the heavens and the earth, followed. This order emphasizes the supremacy of Allah and the interconnectedness of all creation. The prophets, including Muhammad, Ibrahim, and Musa, play vital roles in guiding humanity within this divinely established order. Understanding the creation order is essential for comprehending the Islamic worldview and the relationship between humanity, the divine, and the universe.

location.cosmological: Existing beyond human comprehension, preceding the physical universe and encompassing all of creation. It is not a physical place, but a sequence of events and entities ordained by Allah.

sources.title: The Study Quran: A New Translation and Commentary
sources.author: Seyyed Hossein Nasr
